channel.title: Frontity + Kuberenetes (k8s)
channel.description:
Hello,
I searched through docs and performed a good amount of debugging, so starting this topic is one of my last choices. I'm a fan of Frontity.
I'm making a Kubernetes (k8s) deployment. It seems that running `npx frontity serve` behaives differently in `docker run` vs `kubectl run`. As I understand, `kubectl` completes the container process because the [main thread completes](https://stackoverflow.com/a/31879013/15140630). Does `npx frontity serve` spawn a new thread?
The last line in my Dockerfile is ```CMD ["npx", "--always-spawn=false", "frontity", "serve"]```
Thanks for anyone's input
<!--
Are you asking about "how to solve a specific issue/error/problem in your Frontity project"?
If so, please check this guide on the information we suggest you include in your topic so the community (and the Frontity team) can provide the best possible help as quickly and as efficiently as possible
https://community.frontity.org/t/about-the-dev-talk-questions-category/2/3
The info you may provide: - Description of your issue - Specific errors you’re getting in the terminal or in the browser’s console - A repository with the code of your project - A deployed version of your site - The URL of your WP REST API
-->

item.title: Frontity + Kuberenetes (k8s)
item.dc:creator: ryan_frontityforum
item.description:
Turns out I was doing something pretty dumb - I tagged my image as ‘hello-world’ and didn’t set my imagePullPolicy to ‘Never’. This turned into my red herring because the image that was actually being deployed into my k8s cluster was the official hello-world image.

Doh!

Read full topic

item.title: Frontity + Kuberenetes (k8s)
item.dc:creator: ryan_frontityforum
item.description:
Hello,

I searched through docs and performed a good amount of debugging, so starting this topic is one of my last choices. I’m a fan of Frontity.

I’m making a Kubernetes (k8s) deployment. It seems that running npx frontity serve behaives differently in docker run vs kubectl run. As I understand, kubectl completes the container process because the main thread completes. Does npx frontity serve spawn a new thread?

The last line in my Dockerfile is CMD ["npx", "--always-spawn=false", "frontity", "serve"]

Thanks for anyone’s input

Read full topic
